articles.title: Apache ECharts 6.0: The Powerful Data Visualization Library
articles.description: 12 years on from its first release, ECharts takes another big step forward. Visualization types span from line, bar and pie charts to 3D graphs, calendars and Sankey diagrams. v6 brings an all-new design language, dynamic theme switching, dark mode support, even more chart types, and more. Be sure to enjoy the 100+ demos and the GitHub repo.
articles.author: Apache Software Foundation

articles.title: Announcing TypeScript 5.9
articles.description: One of TypeScript's gentlest steps forward, with support for import defer, --module node20, and ‘expandable hovers’ (below) to see expanded type information in IDEs. We also learn v6.0 will act as a ‘transition point’ to get prepared for the Go-powered ‘native port’ of TypeScript due to arrive as TypeScript 7.0.
articles.author: Microsoft

articles.title: ⚖️ Oracle's Latest Move in the JavaScript™ Trademark Case
articles.description: Oracle’s latest response to Deno’s petition to cancel the JavaScript trademark includes a denial of the assertion that “there is broad industry and public consensus that the term ‘JavaScript’ is generic.” We've been told!
articles.author: United States Patent and Trademark Office

articles.title: ▶  How JavaScript Really Evolves: Inside TC39 with Daniel Ehrenberg
articles.description: A well-recorded 47 minute in-person conversation with one of the most productive members of the TC39 committee covering not just some of the newest features coming to JavaScript, but also the way TC39 works and how you can get involved and propose ideas and help the language move forward.
articles.author: The Weekly Dev's Brew

articles.title: How V8's JSON.stringify Got Over 2x Faster
articles.description: The V8 team has turbo-charged JSON.stringify, giving apps an automatic performance boost for numerous common tasks when using V8 13.8 onwards (such as in Chrome 138). This article unpacks the low-level work behind the speedup.
articles.author: Patrick Thier (V8)

articles.title: "JavaScript Isn’t the Problem, Replacing the Browser Was"
articles.description: The creator of RedwoodSDK, a React framework for building server-side apps on Cloudflare, suggests SPA-style development was a compromise to work around platform limitations, and now a server-first approach makes good sense.
articles.author: Peter Pistorius

articles.title: MathJax v4.0: A JS Display Engine for Math in Browsers
articles.description: Several years in the making, MathJax reasserts its position as one of the best Web-based math rendering approaches. There is, of course, a live demo. v4.0 adds more fonts, a better default font, line-breaking support, ESM support, and much more.
articles.author: Cervone, Sorge, et al.

articles.title: Panda CSS 1.0: Modern, Build-Time, Type-Safe CSS-in-JS
articles.description: A DX-focused CSS-in-JS approach from the creator of Chakra UI that offers build-time generated styles and type safety out of the box. It works with Remix, Vite, Next.js, Astro, and even server components.
articles.author: Segun Adebayo

articles.title: zx v8.8: Write Better Shell Scripts with Node.js
articles.description: A popular way to make shell scripting a more pleasant experience in Node with useful wrappers around child_process, argument escaping, and sensible defaults. v8.8 improves zx’s piping story. (Docs.)
articles.author: Google

articles.title: 🔊 React Native Audio API
articles.description: Get the power and flexibility of the Web Audio API in your React Native apps, whether on iOS, Android or the Web. It's explained in much more detail in this blog post.
articles.author: Software Mansion

articles.description: Sarah Gooding gives an update on the latest proposal advancements at TC39, including Math.sumPrecise, Uint8Array/base64 conversion functions, and Iterator.concat.
A mega roundup of What's New in Viteland covering a variety of Vite ecosystem projects including Vite 7, Rolldown, Oxlint, and Vitest.
SvelteKit has added an experimental remote functions feature – server-side functions that can be called from anywhere within the SvelteKit app.
Vercel now natively supports the deployment of Hono apps. Hono is a lightweight, cross-runtime webapp framework.

articles.title: An Interactive Guide to SVG Paths
articles.description: Hot on the heels of Josh’s excellent introduction to all things SVG comes this detailed look at the powerful <path> element. Josh notes that it’s one of “the most inscrutable” elements to get to grips with, but then demystifies it for us in his usual accessible style.
articles.author: Josh W. Comeau

articles.title: 🗳️ The Results: State of CSS 2025
articles.description: Here are the findings from this year’s CSS-flavored survey, highlighting the fundamental shift this language has taken beyond just styling. A superb real-world look at how we’re all actually using CSS for such things as layout, motion, accessibility and more. (For example, some 80% of all respondents indicated they have now used the :has() pseudo-class.)
articles.author: Devographics

articles.title: Five Useful CSS Functions using the New @function Rule
articles.description: CSS functions recently landed in Chrome 139. So, although browser support is limited right now, that hasn’t stopped Una from sharing a few practical examples of how this powerful feature can be used. This is bound to get you thinking about its capabilities and what this opens up.
articles.author: Una Kravets

articles.title: CSS Properties: A Complete Reference of CSS Properties with Release Dates and Docs Links
articles.description: More of a historical novelty, this is interesting because it allows you to see how many properties were introduced each year. Amazingly, there are 33 properties that are almost 30 years old!
articles.author: Nikolai Shabalin

articles.title: A Gentle Introduction to Anchor Positioning
articles.description: This is a good explainer on how we can now use anchor() to position an element relative to another element using just CSS.
articles.author: Saron Yitbarek (WebKit)

articles.title: CSS-Only Scrollspy Effect using scroll-marker-group and :target-current
articles.description: This new property allows us to recreate the Bootstrap Scrollspy effect in just a few lines — essentially it updates nav links based on a user’s scroll position to indicate which link is currently active in the viewport. Here, Sara shows us how to use it, and how to do so in an accessible and inclusive way.
articles.author: Sara Soueidan

articles.title: The Power of the Intl API: A Definitive Guide to Browser-Native Internationalization
articles.description: Instead of relying on heavy third-party libraries, modern JavaScript now offers the Intl API — a powerful, native way to handle internationalization.
articles.author: Fuqiao Xue

articles.title: 'There’s No Such Thing as a CSS Reset'
articles.description: “The word ‘reset’ implies an objective default state that you’re restoring to, but the only objective default state is what browsers ship. “
articles.author: Adam Stoddard

articles.title: What's My Baseline?
articles.description: The Baseline project aims to help us all better understand feature adoption across the web and the potential risks involved. Now the Chrome team are encouraging us all to share which Baseline target we’re using in our projects.
articles.author: Jeremy Wagner

articles.title: Yellow, Purple and the Myth of “Accessibility Limits Color Palettes”
articles.description: “Accessibility does not limit your color palette choices. What feels limiting is often a lack of knowledge about WCAG color contrast”.
articles.author: Stéphanie Walter

articles.title: ▶  Build Better Websites with the Polypane Development Browser
articles.description: If you’ve ever been curious about developer browser Polypane this hour long video is a good primer, giving a little background on its development, and a walkthrough/demo of all the ways it can be a useful part of your dev toolbelt.
articles.author: Stefan Judis

articles.title: jQuery 4.0.0 Release Candidate 1
articles.description: Version 4.0 of the ever-enduring jQuery has reached the “we think this is ready; now poke it with many sticks” stage. So poke away.
articles.author: Timmy Willison

articles.title: Panda CSS 1.0: Modern, Build-Time, Type-Safe CSS-in-JS
articles.description: A DX-focused CSS-in-JS approach from the creator of Chakra UI that offers build-time generated styles and type safety out of the box. It works with Remix, Vite, Next.js, Astro, and even server components.
articles.author: Segun Adebayo

articles.title: Apache ECharts 6.0: Powerful Data Visualization Library
articles.description: Visualization types span from line, bar and pie charts to 3D, calendars and Sankey diagrams. v6 adopts an all new default theme and design language, adds dynamic theme switching, dark mode support, even more new chart types, and more. Be sure to enjoy the 100+ demos and the GitHub repo.
articles.author: Apache Software Foundation

articles.title: Hoverly: Add On-Hover Effects to Your Links and Headings
articles.description: Designed specifically for WordPress but the overall effects can be used anywhere. Useful for quickly previewing effects like glow, underline slide, text fill, etc.
articles.author: Karol Król

articles.title: PatternCraft: Modern Background Patterns & Gradients
articles.description: Choose from a gallery of gradients, geometric patterns, decorative patterns, or effects – all of which can be live previewed on the page and are easy to copy/paste. There's over 100 here.
articles.author: PatternCraft

articles.title: Kelp: A UI Library Powered by Modern CSS and Web Components
articles.description: Based on class-less HTML for core styles plus utility classes along with Web Components. No build step needed, you can simply customize it using CSS variables and HTML attributes.
articles.author: Chris Ferdinandi

articles.title: Email is 'Easy'
articles.description: A simple quiz in which you have to decide if the displayed email address is valid or not. A fair few of these surprised me.
articles.author: Sam Rose
